---
title: "cho_solve"
generated: true
description: "Solve the SPD system $(a +  lambda I) x = b$ via Cholesky."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.linalg.solve`](../nitrix-linalg-solve.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:cho_solve -->
```python
cho_solve(a: Float[Array, '... n n'], b: Float[Array, '...'], *, l2: float = 0.0) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:cho_solve -->
Solve the SPD system $(a + \lambda I) x = b$ via Cholesky.

The optional `l2` ridge $\lambda$ is added to the diagonal
-- the standard Levenberg-Marquardt / Tikhonov damping -- so a
single call covers both the Gauss-Newton (`l2 = 0`) and
Levenberg-Marquardt (`l2 > 0`) normal-equation solves.  Batches
over any leading dimensions and is reverse-mode differentiable.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:cho_solve -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `a` | `Float` | `...` × `n` × `n` | — | Symmetric positive-definite matrix, `(..., n, n)`. Symmetry is assumed, not enforced. |
| `b` | `Float` | `...` | — | Right-hand side: `(..., n)` (vector) or `(..., n, m)` (matrix of columns). |
| `l2` | `float` | — | `0.0` | Non-negative diagonal ridge added before factoring. Default `0` (plain SPD solve). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. Solution $x$ with the same trailing shape as `b`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:cho_solve -->
::: {.callout-note}
Returns `NaN` if $a + \lambda I$ is not positive-definite
(no Cholesky factor); add an `l2 > 0` ridge if the Gram matrix may
be rank-deficient.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:cho_solve -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
